content:
Is the conversion formula from degrees to radians to be expected of the player? There are other clashes that provide the formula in some form, e.g.:

- https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/122511d0330aadc36ffedba1c21eb2ae126f6b
- https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/128852675264b693c2aefa9c223404e978bfc
- https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/62253a04dc277261d0fa0af73fb1cbb10d89e

The worry is that this clash becomes too dependent on math theory which some clashers might not be familiar with. Some of the wording (e.g. "moving CCW from East") also may not be the most clear.
I think there is an issue with the constraints, it has to be  0 <= angle <= 360. Otherwise you won't have the full unit circle.

Stub generator
* Please add a write statement, e.g. "write answer".

Cases
* Please add a pair of cases where the unrounded radian value has the form of x.x99x…, e.g. when angle is 63 and 126.

Solution
* Please update your solution so that the rounded output correctly shows a trailing zero in the scenario described above.

Thanks.
Makes sense. Thanks for the input!
For that matter, angles greater than 360° can be tested too, but it's really up to the author to choose the range of values to be covered in the contribution.
